evals.prompt: I want to design a de novo protein backbone with RFDiffusion. Generate a protein of around 80-120 residues from scratch with the maximum quality settings. Use the hosted NVIDIA API.
evals.expected_output: A Python script that calls the hosted RFDiffusion endpoint with Bearer auth, contigs set to '80-120' (or similar length range), diffusion_steps=50, passes a non-empty minimal dummy PDB string as input_pdb because live hosted validation requires input_pdb or input_pdb_asset even for de novo generation, and saves the returned PDB file.

assertions.description: Uses the correct hosted RFDiffusion endpoint URL
assertions.check: Script contains 'health.api.nvidia.com/v1/biology/ipd/rfdiffusion/generate'

assertions.description: Sets Authorization header with Bearer token from NGC_API_KEY
assertions.check: Script contains 'Authorization' and 'Bearer' and 'NGC_API_KEY'

assertions.description: contigs field specifies a length range for de novo generation
assertions.check: Script contains 'contigs' with a length-range string like '80-120' or similar (no chain letter prefix — de novo, not motif scaffolding)

assertions.description: diffusion_steps set to 50 (maximum quality)
assertions.check: Script contains 'diffusion_steps' and '50'

assertions.description: Saves the output_pdb from the response to a PDB file
assertions.check: Script accesses 'output_pdb' from the response and writes it to a .pdb file

assertions.description: Passes a non-empty dummy input_pdb for de novo inline generation
assertions.check: Script includes an 'input_pdb' field with a minimal non-empty PDB string such as CRYST1/ATOM/END; it must not omit both input_pdb and input_pdb_asset because live hosted validation requires one of them even for de novo generation

evals.prompt: I have a protein structure (target.pdb) with a functional motif at residues 25-35 of chain A. I want to scaffold this motif — keep those residues and design a new protein structure of 50-80 residues around them. Use the hosted RFDiffusion API.
evals.expected_output: A Python script that reads target.pdb, calls the hosted endpoint with contigs='A25-35/0 50-80' (or equivalent), diffusion_steps=50, and saves the scaffolded backbone PDB.

assertions.description: Uses the correct hosted endpoint URL
assertions.check: Script contains 'health.api.nvidia.com/v1/biology/ipd/rfdiffusion/generate'

assertions.description: Reads target.pdb and passes content as input_pdb
assertions.check: Script reads target.pdb and sends its content as 'input_pdb' in the payload

assertions.description: contigs field preserves chain A residues 25-35
assertions.check: Script contains 'contigs' with a value including 'A25-35' and a generated segment like '50-80'

assertions.description: Contigs uses /0 chain break separator between kept residues and generated segment
assertions.check: Script contigs value contains '/0' as chain break separator

assertions.description: diffusion_steps is set (max is 50)
assertions.check: Script contains 'diffusion_steps' with a value between 1 and 50

assertions.description: Saves output_pdb to a PDB file
assertions.check: Script accesses 'output_pdb' and writes it to a .pdb file

evals.prompt: I want to design a protein binder for my target protein (target.pdb, chain A). The hotspot residues I want the binder to contact are A50, A51, A52, A53, and A54. Design a binder of 50-100 residues. Use the hosted API.
evals.expected_output: A Python script with input_pdb=target.pdb content, contigs referencing chain A of the target and a binder segment, hotspot_res=['A50','A51','A52','A53','A54'], calling the hosted endpoint and saving the generated binder PDB.

assertions.description: Uses the correct hosted endpoint URL
assertions.check: Script contains 'health.api.nvidia.com/v1/biology/ipd/rfdiffusion/generate'

assertions.description: hotspot_res field contains the specified residue identifiers
assertions.check: Script contains 'hotspot_res' with at least some of: 'A50', 'A51', 'A52', 'A53', 'A54'

assertions.description: contigs includes both a target chain reference and a binder generation segment
assertions.check: Script 'contigs' value includes chain A reference and a generated segment separated by '/0'

assertions.description: Reads and submits target.pdb as input_pdb
assertions.check: Script reads target.pdb and passes its content as 'input_pdb'

assertions.description: Saves output_pdb to a PDB file
assertions.check: Script writes 'output_pdb' content to a .pdb file

assertions.description: hotspot_res uses array of strings format like ['A50', 'A51']
assertions.check: Script 'hotspot_res' is a list/array of strings in 'ChainResidue' format

evals.prompt: Help me set up RFDiffusion locally with Docker on my GPU machine. I have an A100 and NGC_API_KEY set. Once it's running, design a de novo protein backbone of 100 residues.
evals.expected_output: Docker setup instructions using shell env first and optional repo-root .env overrides, requiring NGC_API_KEY or NVIDIA_API_KEY fallback plus LOCAL_NIM_CACHE, with the correct image (:2 tag), single GPU flag, cache mount, health check loop, then a local no-auth prediction script targeting localhost:8000 without /v1/ prefix and passing a non-empty dummy input_pdb for de novo generation.

assertions.description: References the correct RFDiffusion container image with :2 tag
assertions.check: Output contains 'nvcr.io/nim/ipd/rfdiffusion' and ':2'

assertions.description: Uses single GPU device specification (device=0)
assertions.check: Output contains '--gpus' and 'device=0'

assertions.description: Local setup uses the repo env contract and LOCAL_NIM_CACHE
assertions.check: Output sources repo-root .env only if present, supports NVIDIA_API_KEY fallback to NGC_API_KEY, requires LOCAL_NIM_CACHE, and mounts LOCAL_NIM_CACHE to /opt/nim/.cache

assertions.description: Includes health check before submitting prediction
assertions.check: Output contains health check against localhost:8000/v1/health/ready

assertions.description: Local inference URL has no /v1/ prefix
assertions.check: Script contains 'localhost:8000/biology/ipd/rfdiffusion/generate'

assertions.description: Local de novo request includes contigs plus required dummy input_pdb
assertions.check: Script contains 'contigs' with a value like '100', 'diffusion_steps', and a non-empty dummy 'input_pdb' string
